---

## jira

## Verification Checklist

- [ ] Prerequisites verified and available
- [ ] Jira operations completed successfully
- [ ] Configuration or output verified against requirements
- [ ] No errors or warnings during execution
- [ ] Changes documented and committed if applicable

## Description

Jira ticket operations via CLI or MCP. Create, view, transition, and manage tickets, sprints, and epics with complex workflows.

## When to Use

- Creating or viewing Jira tickets
- Transitioning tickets between states
- Managing sprints and backlogs
- Working with epics
- Automating ticket operations
- Bulk ticket management
- Integrating Jira with workflows

## When NOT to Use

- Simple web interface usage
- Non-Jira issue tracking
- Real-time collaboration
- Exploratory work without tickets

## Workflow

### Phase 1: Identify Ticket

- Find or create Jira ticket
- Review ticket details
- Understand requirements

### Phase 2: Manage Ticket

- Update ticket fields
- Add comments or attachments
- Link related tickets
- Assign to team member

### Phase 3: Transition State

- Move ticket through workflow
- Update status
- Resolve or close ticket

### Phase 4: Track & Report

- Monitor ticket progress
- Generate reports
- Archive completed work

## Tools & References

- **Related Skills**: work-on-ticket, task-management
- **Jira CLI**: Command-line interface
- **Issue Keys**: Format like PROJ-123
- **Workflows**: Custom transitions and states

## Best Practices

- Use descriptive ticket titles
- Include acceptance criteria
- Link related tickets
- Update status regularly
- Add comments for context
- Use labels and components
- Archive old tickets
